title: cuscuta australis var. tineoi
intro: Cuscuta australis var. tineoi, also known as Dodder, is a type of parasitic plant that attaches itself to other plants to obtain nutrients. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. Cuscuta australis var. tineoi has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used topically as a poultice or compress to aid in wound healing.
